text: Conditional GANs: The conditional GAN was developed by Mirza & Osindero (2014), the auxiliary classifier GAN by Odena et al. (2017), and the InfoGAN by Chen et al. (2016b). The discriminator of these models usually append the conditional information to the dis

Image translation: Isola et al. (2017) developed the Pix2Pix algorithm (figure 15.16), and a similar system with higher-resolution results was subsequently developed by Wang et al. (2018b). StarGAN (Choi et al., 2018) performs image-to-image translation across

Adversarial loss: In many image translation tasks, there is no “generator”; these can be considered supervised learning tasks with an adversarial loss that encourages realism. The super-resolution algorithm of Ledig et al. (2017) is a good example of this (fig

Inverting GANs: One way to edit real images is to project them into the latent space, manipulate the latent variable, and then re-project them to image space. This process is known as resynthesis. Unfortunately, GANs only map from the latent variable to the ob

There has been particular interest in inversion for StyleGAN because it produces excellent results and can control the image at different scales. Unfortunately, Abdal et al. (2020) showed that it is not possible to invert StyleGAN without artifacts and propose
Editing images with GANs: The iGAN (Zhu et al., 2016) allows users to make interactive edits by scribbling or warping parts of an existing image. The tool then adjusts the output
